node.statement: N1.3 (PARTIAL SUMS, pure algebra + linearity of j). Let a_n be the fixed (-1/2)-binomial Taylor coefficients of f(x)=x^{-1/2} about x0=1 (def-spectral-idempotent / GT-taylor-calculus), and define the partial sums g_N(S_F^2) := a_0 I_A + sum_{n=1}^{N} a_n (S_F^2 - I_A)^n in A and g_N(S_Phi^2) := a_0 I_B + sum_{n=1}^{N} a_n (S_Phi^2 - I_B)^n in B (the SAME coefficients a_n in both — theta is one fixed power series, def-spectral-idempotent). Then for every N, g_N(S_F^2) j = j g_N(S_Phi^2). DERIVATION: g_N(S_F^2) j = (a_0 I_A + sum_{n=1}^{N} a_n (S_F^2 - I_A)^n) j = a_0 (I_A j) + sum_{n=1}^{N} a_n ((S_F^2 - I_A)^n j) [composition distributes over the finite sum and scalars on the A-side — bilinearity of composition of linear maps, GT-hyp]. By node 1.2, (S_F^2 - I_A)^n j = j (S_Phi^2 - I_B)^n, and I_A j = j I_B (N1.1); so = a_0 (j I_B) + sum_{n=1}^{N} a_n (j (S_Phi^2 - I_B)^n) = j (a_0 I_B + sum_{n=1}^{N} a_n (S_Phi^2 - I_B)^n) [LINEARITY of j, GT-hyp H3: j pulls the finite linear combination out] = j g_N(S_Phi^2). Hence g_N(S_F^2) j = j g_N(S_Phi^2) for every finite N. Uses node 1.2, def-spectral-idempotent, GT-hyp.
